# ADR-260032: Dual-View Delivery, Validation, and Rollout Baseline

## Workflow

- This ADR records the accepted delivery and validation baseline for the dual-view feature.
- It defines required test layers, performance targets, and feature-flag rollout/rollback strategy.
- This ADR refines ADR-260028 through ADR-260031 by adding release governance constraints.

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-12
Owners: Repository owner, product collaborator

## Context

The dual-view model introduces UI/state complexity that can regress quickly without explicit coverage and release constraints. A concrete validation baseline and controlled rollout strategy are required before implementation starts.

## Decision

### D1: Required Test Layers
- Unit coverage includes:
  - view-tab state transitions
  - shared selection synchronization logic
  - large-graph threshold gating logic
  - keyboard handlers (`Tab`, arrows, `Enter`/`Space`, `Esc`)
- Integration coverage includes:
  - shared dataset parity between views
  - cross-view mutation reflection behavior
  - degrade-mode transitions at 150 and 400 thresholds
- E2E coverage includes:
  - login to default Network view to search to view-switch flow
  - selection carryover across views
  - >400 warning-and-continue gate path
  - keyboard-only navigation path across shell/tab/graph/panels

### D2: Performance Targets
- <=150 nodes: p95 view-switch visual update <= 200ms
- 151-400 nodes (reduced mode): p95 view-switch visual update <= 400ms
- >400 nodes: render is gated by explicit confirm; after continue, first paint target <= 1.5s (baseline desktop profile)

### D3: Rollout Strategy
- Release behind feature flag: `networkViewEnabled`
- Default flag posture:
  - dev/staging: on
  - production: configurable with safe fallback
- Flow view remains available as immediate fallback path.
- Rollback rule: disable `networkViewEnabled` if early rollout regression threshold is exceeded.

## Alternatives Considered

- Option A: Direct release with no flag. Rejected: rollback friction too high.
- Option B: Unit tests only for MVP. Rejected: misses cross-layer behavioral regressions.
- Option C: No explicit performance SLO targets. Rejected: no objective acceptance boundary.

## Consequences

### Positive
- Clear quality gates for implementation and review.
- Objective performance and rollback criteria.
- Safer production rollout with immediate fallback.

### Negative
- Increased testing and instrumentation effort.
- Potentially longer delivery cycle per slice.

### Risks
- Baseline desktop profile may need calibration by environment.
- If metrics are noisy, strict p95 thresholds may require tolerance tuning.

## Verification

- Verify required unit/integration/E2E suites exist and pass.
- Verify performance measurements against D2 thresholds.
- Verify feature flag can disable Network view without redeploy.
- Verify Flow fallback remains operational when flag is off.

## Rollback

- Primary rollback: set `networkViewEnabled` to off.
- Secondary rollback: restrict to Flow view while preserving recorded governance contracts.
